perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties to this Agreement intends to disclose information (the Confidential Information) to the other party for the purpose of [insert details e.g. discussing the possibility of the parties entering into a joint venture] (the Purpose).
perturbation.changed_text:
Signed [by [insert name]] OR [on behalf of][insert name] by its duly authorised representative]: _____________________________ Signature _____________________________ Name _____________________________ Position Signed [by [insert name]] OR [on behalf of] [insert name] by its duly authorised representative]: _____________________________ Signature ________________________ _____ Name _____________________________ Position
Each of the parties to this Agreement intends to disclose information (the Confidential Information) to the other party for the purpose of [insert details e.g. discussing the possibility of the parties entering into a joint venture] (the Purpose).
perturbation.explanation: Moving the definition of the agreement at the very end will make it so people signing may not know what they are signing.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law (General Principles of Contract Formation)
perturbation.law_explanation: Basic contract law requires that there be clear and unambiguous offer and acceptance. Putting the agreement definition at the very end undermines this principle as the parties signatures confirm an agreement before the purpose is even disclosed or understood. This could make the contract unenforceable due to lack of clear intent and consideration.

perturbation.justification: The moved definition creates a structural flaw that leads to a legal contradiction. The Law Explanation states that placing the agreement definition at the end undermines the basic contract law principle of clear offer and acceptance. As such, the altered text contradicts legal standards for contract formation, making the agreement potentially unenforceable, according to the Law Explanation. The definition provided states that this type of structural flaw leads to a legal contradiction when 'a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret.'
